disclaimer: All lever parameters in this file — including phasing_curve, cta_rate, sustainability_score, bounce_back_risk, base_execution_probability, and implementation_weeks — are derived from internal consulting engagement analysis across 40+ Indian and global enterprise cost reduction programmes (FY2020-25). No single published external study sources these specific parametric values. Parameters are calibrated against directional outcomes from McKinsey, KPMG, and Deloitte cost transformation engagements where published case data was available. Do not cite these parameters as externally-validated industry benchmarks in client deliverables. Use as internal diagnostic ranges only.
source_basis: Cross-engagement calibration: 40+ Indian mid-large enterprise cost reduction programmes (₹500Cr–₹50,000Cr revenue). Implementation timelines validated against Hackett Group procurement transformation benchmarks (directional) and McKinsey Operations cost reduction case studies (directional). Phasing curves reflect observed cash-realisation patterns from internal programme tracking.

condition_precedents: Headcount > 500 (viable SSC scale)
Governance model and service charter defined
HR/union consultation completed

execution_playbook.step: Map F&A (AP, AR, GL, Payroll) and HR ops processes by BU; measure volume, FTE, and cost per transaction for each BU
Design SSC operating model: process scope, location, SLA framework, governance, and pricing/chargeback model
Migrate Wave 1 (transactional F&A) with parallel run for one period; transition staff under TUPE-equivalent arrangement
Stabilise SSC with SLA dashboard; enforce chargeback to BUs; migrate Wave 2 (HR ops, IT service desk)

diagnostic_signals.signal: Same transactional process (e.g., invoice processing) performed by >3 independent BU teams at different cost per transaction
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Consolidation will reduce unit cost through scale and standardisation

diagnostic_signals.signal: Cost per invoice processed >₹150 vs SSC benchmark <₹80 for equivalent volume
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Scale economies from SSC are material

diagnostic_signals.signal: Multiple ERP instances or process variants across BUs for the same transactional sub-process

required_data_fields: Process inventory with volume, FTE, and cost per transaction by BU
Current chargeback or recharge model for shared services
SLA benchmark data for comparable SSC operations
Location cost analysis (talent, real estate, infrastructure) for SSC hub

condition_precedents: Labor cost differential analysis completed
Quality / attrition risk assessed for target location
Transition management office established

execution_playbook.step: Confirm scope for Store Network Rationalisation: validate baseline and stakeholders
Model span/layers and target FTE envelope
Execute change plan with legal/ER review
Track attrition, productivity, and cost-to-serve

diagnostic_signals.signal: Span of control or cost/employee above benchmark

condition_precedents: Contract compliance audit completed
Rebate entitlement schedule reconciled
Vendor deduction process established in AP

condition_precedents: License utilisation audit completed (usage data ≥ 90 days)
Over-provisioned tiers identified and downgrade path confirmed with vendor
IT asset management (ITAM) tooling deployed

execution_playbook.step: Deploy SAM (Software Asset Management) tool to scan all endpoints and servers; reconcile entitlements vs active usage over 90 days
Identify over-licensed users (named users with zero usage in 90 days) and tier mismatches (full licence where reader/viewer suffices)
Reclaim over-licensed seats before next renewal; right-size tier mix in renewal negotiation with vendor (Microsoft, Oracle, SAP, Salesforce)
Implement continuous licence monitoring; gate new seat requests through SAM tool; track licence spend per head monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Software licence spend per employee >₹80,000/year with >30% of seats showing zero or minimal usage in past 90 days
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Rightsizing will yield direct licence spend reduction at next renewal

diagnostic_signals.signal: Full-access licences issued to users who only consume reports or dashboards, where cheaper viewer tier exists
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Tier rationalisation will reduce per-seat cost without impacting productivity

diagnostic_signals.signal: Multi-year licence agreements expiring within 12 months with no renegotiation underway
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Renewal window is open for rightsizing negotiation

required_data_fields: Licence entitlement data by software title, user, and tier
Active usage data (logins, feature usage) by user over 90 days
Contract renewal dates and pricing tiers by vendor
Vendor audit exposure and licence true-up obligations

condition_precedents: Cloud cost visibility tooling deployed (e.g. CloudHealth, Apptio)
Tagging taxonomy enforced for cost allocation
Reserved instance / savings plan commitment approved by finance

execution_playbook.step: Enable cloud cost discovery: tag all resources by BU, project, and environment; implement cost allocation dashboard by AWS/Azure/GCP billing account
Identify top-5 cost drivers: idle/underutilised resources, oversized instances, unattached volumes, and unused reserved instances
Right-size running instances and migrate to modern SKUs; purchase reserved instances or savings plans for steady-state workloads
Implement automated shutdown for non-prod environments outside business hours; enforce budget alerts and FinOps governance monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Cloud resources with CPU/memory utilisation <20% on average representing >30% of total cloud spend
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Rightsizing will reduce cloud spend without impacting workload performance

diagnostic_signals.signal: On-demand pricing paid for >60% of steady-state workloads (no reserved instances or savings plans)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Commitment purchase will reduce on-demand premium by 30-40%

diagnostic_signals.signal: Non-production environments running 24/7 with weekend and off-hours utilisation <10%
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automated shutdown will reduce non-prod cloud spend by 40-60%

required_data_fields: Cloud billing data by account, service, and resource (3 months)
Resource utilisation data (CPU, memory, storage) by instance
Tagging hygiene report and resource inventory
Reserved instance and savings plan coverage and utilisation
Non-production environment inventory and usage pattern

condition_precedents: Rate card benchmarked against market (offshore, nearshore, onshore)
Statement of work vs. time-and-material mix reviewed
Preferred supplier panel rationalised

execution_playbook.step: Classify all subcontractor spend: T&M vs fixed-price, offshore vs onshore, and vendor tier; calculate effective bill rate and margin by vendor
Assess Statement of Work quality: identify T&M contracts that should be fixed-price given defined scope; convert and enforce milestone billing
Rationalise vendor panel: concentrate volume in top-5 strategic partners for improved pricing and SLA leverage; exit tail vendors
Track subcontractor spend as % of project cost, effective bill rate by skill category, and vendor SLA compliance monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Subcontractor spend >35% of total project cost with >60% on T&M basis for work with defined scope
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Fixed-price conversion and bill-rate renegotiation will reduce subcontractor cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Effective bill rate for subcontractor roles 20% above internal FTE equivalent cost for similar skills
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Renegotiation or in-house replacement will reduce cost per skilled FTE

diagnostic_signals.signal: Vendor panel >30 subcontractors with bottom-half contributing <10% of project delivery
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Consolidation will improve pricing leverage and reduce vendor management overhead

required_data_fields: Subcontractor spend by vendor, project, and contract type (T&M vs fixed-price)
Bill rate by vendor and skill category vs internal FTE cost equivalent
Vendor performance data (SLA compliance, defect rate, on-time delivery)
Statement of Work quality assessment by contract
Internal bench vs subcontractor skills overlap analysis

condition_precedents: IoT / sensor data available for critical assets
Maintenance scheduling system (CMMS) integrated
Technician training on condition-based protocols completed

execution_playbook.step: Rank assets by criticality (availability impact × replacement cost); instrument top-20 critical assets with vibration, temperature, and oil sensors
Build ML predictive models (anomaly detection, remaining useful life) using historian and CMMS data; validate on holdout failure events
Integrate PdM predictions into CMMS (SAP PM/IBM Maximo); shift maintenance triggers from calendar to condition-based
Track OEE improvement, unplanned-outage reduction, and maintenance cost per MW; rebaseline after 6 months

diagnostic_signals.signal: Unplanned outage rate >5% of planned generation hours for critical rotating equipment
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Condition-based maintenance will reduce forced outage cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Preventive maintenance cost >30% of total maintenance budget with >40% of PMs yielding no defect found
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Optimising PM frequency and shifting to PdM will reduce unnecessary maintenance

diagnostic_signals.signal: Mean time between failures declining YoY for pumps, compressors, or transformers
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Asset degradation trend justifies condition-monitoring investment

required_data_fields: Asset criticality register with replacement cost and availability impact
CMMS maintenance history (work orders, failure codes, costs) ≥3 years
Historian/SCADA data for instrumented assets (vibration, temperature, pressure)
Current PM frequency schedule and inspection outcomes
Unplanned outage log with duration and production impact

mode_shift_logistics.lever_name: Logistics Mode Shift (Air → Road / Rail / Ocean)
condition_precedents: Lead time analysis confirms alternative mode feasible
Customer SLA / service level impact assessed and accepted
Carrier contracts updated for new modes

execution_playbook.step: Baseline freight spend by mode (road, rail, air, sea) and lane; calculate cost per tonne-km and transit time by mode
Model modal shift feasibility by lane: rail vs road for >800 km lanes, coastal shipping for port-to-port, air-to-surface for non-urgent loads
Negotiate long-term contracts with rail (CONCOR/private) and coastal shipping operators; optimise carrier mix per lane
Track freight cost per tonne-km, modal split, transit time, and OTIF by lane monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Air or premium express freight >10% of total freight spend for non-time-sensitive loads
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Mode shift to surface/rail will reduce freight cost without impacting service

diagnostic_signals.signal: Road freight on lanes >800 km where rail alternative exists and service level is acceptable
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Rail or coastal modal shift will reduce cost per tonne-km by 30-50%

diagnostic_signals.signal: Freight cost as % of net sales trending upward for ≥3 consecutive quarters
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Structural review of logistics network and mode mix is required

required_data_fields: Freight spend by mode, lane, and carrier (12 months)
Volume (tonnes) and transit time by lane and mode
Rail/coastal shipping network connectivity to key origin-destination pairs
Carrier rate cards and contract terms by mode
Inventory holding cost and service-level trade-off data

condition_precedents: Demand-based scheduling system deployed
Labour standards (tasks per hour) established
Manager training on scheduling tool completed

execution_playbook.step: Conduct time-and-motion study at representative stores: measure labour hours by department (cashier, replenishment, customer service) vs transaction volume
Implement workforce management software with footfall-linked demand forecasting and automated shift generation by store
Convert fixed-schedule staff to flexible/part-time model in high-footfall-variance stores; negotiate new employment terms with union where applicable
Track labour cost as % of store revenue, transactions per labour hour, and overtime % monthly by store format

diagnostic_signals.signal: Labour cost as % of store revenue >12% vs organised retail benchmark of <9% for similar format
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Demand-linked scheduling will reduce excess labour cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Labour hours in low-footfall hours (weekday mornings, late evenings) scheduled at same staffing as peak hours
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Footfall-linked scheduling will reduce wasted labour hours

diagnostic_signals.signal: Overtime as % of total labour cost >10% consistently, indicating demand forecasting gap
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Improved scheduling will reduce overtime premium

required_data_fields: Hourly footfall data by store and day type (weekday, weekend, festival)
Labour hours by department and shift pattern
Labour cost per store (permanent, part-time, casual)
Transactions per hour by department and store format
Union contract terms and flexibility provisions

condition_precedents: Claims data quality validated (> 90% structured fields)
AI triage model trained and tested on historical claims
Regulatory approval obtained for automated settlement thresholds

execution_playbook.step: Map claims journey by product line: measure FNOL-to-settlement cycle time, manual touchpoints, and STP rate by claim type and value band
Design STP pathway by claim type: auto-adjudication rules for low-value/low-complexity claims; integrate with surveyor and hospital network
Deploy FNOL automation (WhatsApp/IVR), triage scoring model, and digital document collection; eliminate manual data entry at intake
Track STP rate, claims processing cost per claim, and customer satisfaction (NPS/CSAT) by product line monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Claims STP rate <30% for product lines where IRDAI allows straight-through processing (motor own-damage, health cashless)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: STP implementation will materially reduce cost per claim and settlement time

diagnostic_signals.signal: Claims processing cost per claim >₹800 for motor or >₹1,200 for health claims vs peer benchmark
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation will reduce claims processing cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Average claims settlement time >15 days for cashless health or >30 days for motor own-damage
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Process automation will improve settlement speed and customer satisfaction

required_data_fields: Claims journey map with volume, STP rate, and TAT by product line and claim type
Claims processing cost per claim by product (FTE cost + surveyor + system)
FNOL data: channel mix (agent, digital, call centre) and data completeness at intake
Policy admin system and claims management system integration architecture
IRDAI compliance requirements for automated adjudication

condition_precedents: Smart meter / AMI data available for loss mapping
Network topology updated in GIS system
Field enforcement programme for energy theft established

execution_playbook.step: Conduct AT&C loss audit by distribution zone/feeder: segregate technical (I²R, core) losses from commercial losses (theft, metering error)
Deploy smart meters and AMI on high-loss feeders; automate DT-level loss monitoring and tamper alerts
Execute technical loss reduction projects (conductor reconductoring, capacitor banks, DT loss reduction) on top-10 loss feeders
Enforce anti-theft drive (inspection, metering audit, legal action) for commercial loss feeders >20% loss level
Track AT&C loss monthly by zone; recalibrate DISCOM targets per SERC mandate

diagnostic_signals.signal: AT&C loss >15% of energy input indicating material technical or commercial loss above SERC benchmark
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Loss-reduction projects will yield direct cost savings and revenue recovery

diagnostic_signals.signal: Feeder-level loss variance >10 percentage points across distribution zones for similar feeder types
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Targeted interventions on high-loss feeders will disproportionately reduce system losses

diagnostic_signals.signal: Metering error or billing gap >5% of units sold on high-revenue commercial feeders
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Metering and billing accuracy correction will recover commercial losses

required_data_fields: Feeder-level energy sent and billed data (12 months)
DT loss audit data by distribution transformer
Conductor specifications and age profile for high-loss feeders
Smart meter installation status and AMI coverage by zone
SERC-mandated AT&C loss reduction targets by year

condition_precedents: GST reconciliation between GSTR-2A and purchase register completed
Reverse charge mechanism (RCM) liabilities identified
GST consultant / ERP configuration validated

execution_playbook.step: Reconcile GSTR-2A/2B (supplier invoices filed) vs GSTR-3B (ITC claimed) for all returns periods; identify unclaimed ITC by GST head
Investigate ITC gaps: misclassified blocked credits, supplier non-filing, and wrong GST head mapping in ERP; quantify recoverable amount
File DRC-03 or revised GSTR-3B for recoverable credits; engage with vendors who have not filed returns to resolve mismatch
Implement monthly auto-reconciliation in ERP/GSTN portal; update chart of accounts to correctly classify ITC-eligible expenses

diagnostic_signals.signal: ITC claimed as % of total GST paid <80% for input-intensive sectors (manufacturing, construction, logistics) indicating under-claim
diagnostic_signals.confirms: ITC recovery will yield direct cash refund or credit

diagnostic_signals.signal: GSTR-2A/2B vs GSTR-3B ITC mismatch >5% of eligible ITC pool
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Reconciliation exercise will identify recoverable amount

diagnostic_signals.signal: Blocked credit (Schedule II items) incorrectly claimed or ITC-eligible items incorrectly coded in ERP as non-ITC
diagnostic_signals.confirms: GL reclassification will unlock additional ITC

required_data_fields: GSTR-2A/2B data vs GSTR-3B ITC claim (all periods under review)
GL coding for input costs by GST head (ITC eligible vs blocked)
Vendor GSTIN compliance status and filing history
ERP GST configuration and tax code mapping
Prior-year tax authority ITC mismatch notices if any

condition_precedents: Network inventory accurately mapped in OSS/NMS
Maintenance vendor contracts reviewed for performance SLAs
Energy management programme initiated for passive infrastructure

execution_playbook.step: Disaggregate network OPEX by cost driver: passive infrastructure (power, rent), active network (maintenance, spares), and managed services; benchmark vs revenue
Identify top-5 cost reduction levers: tower power cost reduction, network equipment refresh, RAN-sharing, NOC automation, and spares pooling
Execute quick wins (automated energy management, spares pooling); develop business case for medium-term investments (equipment refresh, RAN sharing)
Track network OPEX as % of revenue and EBITDA, and cost per subscriber monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Network OPEX as % of service revenue >25% vs peer operator benchmark of <22%
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Structural OPEX reduction will improve EBITDA margin

diagnostic_signals.signal: Power cost >35% of total passive infrastructure OPEX with diesel backup >20% of power mix
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Energy management and green energy transition will reduce power cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Managed service contract for RAN management not benchmarked for >3 years
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Managed service rebid will improve pricing and SLA

required_data_fields: Network OPEX breakdown by cost category and geography
Power consumption data: grid vs diesel ratio per tower/BTS
Network equipment age profile and maintenance cost
RAN managed service contract terms and SLA performance
TRAI network performance indicators (CAGR trends, EBITDA benchmarks)

condition_precedents: Protocol complexity review completed
Site selection criteria optimised for enrolment velocity
CRO contract renegotiated on performance basis

execution_playbook.step: Audit active clinical trial portfolio: assess protocol complexity, site enrolment rate, CRO performance, and cost per patient enrolled vs benchmark
Redesign protocol for top-3 delayed studies: simplify endpoints, adopt decentralised trial (DCT) elements (eConsent, remote monitoring), and use risk-based monitoring
Renegotiate CRO contracts: shift from FTE-based to milestone-based pricing; consolidate to preferred CRO panel (2–3 vendors)
Track cost per patient enrolled, enrolment velocity, and protocol deviation rate by study monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Cost per patient enrolled >30% above CRO industry benchmark for comparable therapeutic area and Phase
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Protocol simplification and CRO renegotiation will reduce per-patient cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Average patient enrolment rate <80% of plan, extending study timelines and increasing fixed trial costs
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Site optimisation and DCT elements will improve enrolment velocity

diagnostic_signals.signal: CRO panel >5 vendors with no preferred vendor agreement or performance benchmarking
diagnostic_signals.confirms: CRO consolidation will improve pricing leverage and management efficiency

required_data_fields: Active clinical trial list with status, cost to date, and estimated cost to complete
Cost per patient enrolled vs industry benchmark by therapeutic area
CRO performance data (enrolment rate, protocol deviations, budget adherence)
CTMS data: site performance, screen fail rates, and enrolment milestones

condition_precedents: Group-level mandate from Group CEO / Board
Service charter and SLA framework agreed with all BUs
HR / IR clearance for role transitions obtained

execution_playbook.step: Assess process maturity and standardisation readiness across F&A, HR, and IT sub-processes; identify SSC candidate scope by BU
Design SSC operating model: location, governance charter, SLA framework, and chargeback model
Execute migration in waves—Wave 1 transactional F&A (AP/AR/GL), Wave 2 HR operations, Wave 3 IT service desk
Stabilise SSC with SLA monitoring dashboard; enforce chargeback to business units to drive demand discipline

diagnostic_signals.signal: Duplicate F&A or HR back-office teams across >3 business units performing identical transactional processes
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Consolidation will eliminate redundancy and reduce per-transaction cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Cost per transaction (e.g., cost per invoice processed) >2× SSC benchmark for same process
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Scale economies from SSC are material

diagnostic_signals.signal: Multiple ERP instances or process variants across BUs for the same transactional process
diagnostic_signals.confirms: SSC standardisation will reduce technology and training cost

required_data_fields: F&A, HR, and IT process inventory with volume, FTE, and cost per BU
Chargeback/recharge model and inter-company billing data
Location analysis for SSC hub (cost, talent, infrastructure)

condition_precedents: Span-and-layer analysis completed
Role redesign approved by CHRO
Lean corporate centre operating model defined

execution_playbook.step: Build ZBB baseline for all corporate overhead cost lines; map every FTE and spend item to business-justification owner
Benchmark corporate overhead as % of group revenue against listed-peer conglomerates; identify gap to top-quartile
Define elimination and reduction targets by function; model span-of-control and layer count vs benchmark
Execute reduction plan with HR/legal review; enforce new span and layer guardrails in org design
Track overhead cost as % of revenue quarterly; gate new corporate hiring against overhead budget

diagnostic_signals.signal: Corporate overhead as % of group revenue >3% vs peer conglomerate median of <2%
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Structural overhead reduction opportunity is material

diagnostic_signals.signal: Corporate headcount growing faster than group revenue over past 3 years
Management layers at group HQ >6 from CEO to first-line manager

required_data_fields: Full corporate overhead P&L by function and cost line
Headcount by function and layer at group HQ
Peer benchmark: corporate overhead % of revenue for listed conglomerates
Group revenue and EBITDA trend (3 years)
Org chart with span-of-control data by function

condition_precedents: Benefits cost audit completed (healthcare, PF, D&O, group life split by benefit type)
Actuarial review of self-funded vs. fully-insured health plan completed
HR and legal sign-off on benefit plan design changes

execution_playbook.step: Confirm scope for Loyalty Programme Cost Efficiency: validate baseline and stakeholders
Define target operating policy and approval workflow
Implement system controls and communicate standards
Monitor compliance and reset budgets to hold savings

diagnostic_signals.signal: Maverick or off-catalog spend share elevated
Policy exceptions rising period-on-period
